Temperature products for the European Northwest Shelf/

Ocean

When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated and a row is added to this table.  The 
third column specifies which sections or sub -sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the version of the product to which the change applies.

KEY DEFINITIONS 
Foundation SST Foundation Sea Surface Temperature (Foundation SST)  refers 
to the temperature of the uppermost layer of the ocean  
(typically at a depth of a few centimetres to several meters) 
that is not affected by diurnal warming or surface cooling  due 
to solar radiation or wind mixing. It represents the baseline or 
background sea surface temperature, usually estimated during 
the night-time or under well-mixed conditions, making it more 
stable and suitable for climate studies and model assimilation. 
adjusted_sea_surface_temperature daily (nighttime), satellite multi -sensor composite of 
foundation sea surface temperature (SST) in L3S products. 
analysed_sst Gap-free daily foundation sea surface temperature (SST) in L4 
products, interpolated from L3S SSTs.
